judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports hit all four expectations with strong repo-grounded evidence; Report 2 is marginally more explicit on the reproducibility check (re-run twice) but they are comparable.

report_1.evidence: POST to a real model endpoint (`https://api.scorelab-models.example.com/v1/score`) with a bearer token read from `os.environ['SCORELAB_API_KEY']`; the module docstring says outright they "hit the real model endpoint and incur per-call cost." ... Leave them deselected; "2 deselected" is the designed, healthy state of the default run.

report_2.evidence: They make real HTTP POSTs to `https://api.scorelab-models.example.com/v1/score` with a bearer token from `SCORELAB_API_KEY`, and the module docstring states they "hit the real model endpoint and incur per-call cost." ... the deselection is intentional cost-gating, not a coverage gap — removing `-m "not live"` would make every default test run (local and CI) spend money

report_1.evidence: then read the resulting diff of `tests/golden/expected_scores.json` line by line and confirm every changed value is explained by the reweight ... confidence `0.812 → 0.81` comes solely from the rounding change, which looks like an accidental rider ... Regenerating first, as the teammate proposed, would launder the unintended rounding change into the project documentation

report_2.evidence: read the golden diff hunk-by-hunk and confirm each changed value against hand-computed expectations ... A regenerated golden would change every `confidence` value (0.812→0.81, 0.444→0.44, 0.633→0.63) for a reason that doesn't trace to the stated intent — that's a precision regression being laundered into the project documentation

report_1.evidence: I would not cite the figures from `docs/testing.md`, because both are wrong against the repository's current state; I would source the numbers from fresh tool output instead. ... flag `docs/testing.md` as stale rather than quoting it.

report_2.evidence: Do not cite the numbers from `docs/testing.md` — both are stale; re-measure and report fresh numbers with their provenance.

report_1.evidence: One mechanical caveat: `update_golden.py` iterates a `set` (`CATEGORIES`), so key order in the regenerated JSON is nondeterministic across runs — the values are fine, but expect spurious ordering churn in the file diff.

report_2.evidence: re-run the suite in plain assert mode at least twice after regenerating to confirm the new project documentation is deterministic; and note that `scripts/update_golden.py` iterates a Python `set` (`CATEGORIES`), whose ordering is arbitrary, so the regenerated file's key order can differ between runs
